name: SK Hynix
thesis: The stock remains the most direct Korean HBM beneficiary if AI infrastructure spending persists through 2026.
bull_points: HBM capacity expansion maps directly to hyperscaler AI demand.
Pricing power is stronger in high-end memory than in commodity DRAM.
Recent US capex headlines reinforce medium-term revenue visibility.
risk_points: Near-term valuation already reflects strong demand assumptions.
Execution risk remains on output mix and customer qualification.
Any slowdown in GPU server deployment can delay the thesis.
checkpoints: HBM shipment guidance in the next earnings call.
Customer capex commentary from Nvidia and hyperscalers.
Signs of margin expansion rather than only revenue growth.

industry_links.reason: Core producer with leading Korean capacity.
Memory cycle recovery and AI server mix.

name: Samsung Electronics
thesis: Samsung is a broader memory and foundry vehicle with upside if HBM execution improves.
bull_points: Broad memory cycle recovery supports baseline earnings.
HBM and packaging improvements can expand AI relevance.
Balance sheet strength reduces downside from slower macro conditions.
risk_points: HBM competitiveness is less proven than the market leader.
The stock can trade sideways if AI upside stays conceptual.

industry_links.reason: Strategic product area with execution upside.

name: S-Oil
thesis: Oil-linked volatility keeps the name tactical rather than conviction-led.
bull_points: Crude spikes can lift near-term refinery attention.
Macro headlines create trading interest quickly.
A stable demand backdrop can support refining spreads.
risk_points: Input cost volatility can reverse sentiment fast.
Macro benefit is weaker than direct AI or structural growth themes.
The thesis depends heavily on commodity moves outside company control.

industry_links.reason: Direct sensitivity to crude and refining margins.
